name: wp-block-themes
description: Use when developing WordPress block themes: theme.json (global settings/styles), templates and template parts, patterns, style variations, and Site Editor troubleshooting (style hierarchy, overrides, caching).
capabilities: Detect block themes and locate theme.json files in repositories
Guide theme.json configuration for settings and styles
Create and edit HTML templates and template parts
Debug style hierarchy and Site Editor issues
author: Automattic
securitySummary: This skill provides documentation and guidance for WordPress block theme development. The only executable script (detect_block_themes.mjs) safely reads theme.json files using bounded filesystem operations with no network access or command execution. All 92 static findings are false positives: markdown backticks are misinterpreted as shell execution, documentation URLs as network calls, and JSON content hashes as C2 indicators.
valueStatement: WordPress block themes use theme.json for global settings and styles. This skill provides expert guidance for theme.json configuration, template creation, pattern management, and debugging styling issues in the Site Editor.
userTitle: Develop WordPress block themes with expert guidance
limitations: Does not modify theme files directly; provides guidance only
Requires access to the theme directory on the filesystem
Cannot test changes in a live WordPress environment

useCases.title: Configure theme.json
useCases.description: Set up global settings, presets, typography, colors, and per-block styles in theme.json

useCases.title: Create block templates
useCases.description: Build HTML templates and template parts for block-based WordPress themes

useCases.title: Debug styling issues
useCases.description: Resolve styles not applying, editor mismatch, and style hierarchy problems

promptTemplates.title: Detect block themes
promptTemplates.prompt: Run detect_block_themes to find all theme.json files in this repository and identify which are block themes

promptTemplates.title: Review theme.json
promptTemplates.prompt: Review the theme.json file at [PATH] and suggest improvements for settings and styles

promptTemplates.title: Create new template
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new template file at templates/[NAME].html following WordPress block theme conventions

promptTemplates.title: Debug style issues
promptTemplates.prompt: Debug why styles from theme.json are not appearing in the Site Editor. Check style hierarchy and user overrides

bestPractices: Run detect_block_themes to verify the correct theme root before making changes
Understand style hierarchy: core defaults → theme.json → child theme → user customizations
Test changes in the Site Editor and frontend to confirm styles apply correctly
antiPatterns: Editing theme.json without understanding the style hierarchy
Creating templates without checking existing patterns and conventions
Activating a new theme without verifying theme.json configuration

faq.question: What WordPress versions are supported?
faq.answer: Targets WordPress 6.9+ with theme.json schema version 3. Earlier versions use different schema versions.

faq.question: What are theme.json versions?
faq.answer: theme.json has schema versions. Version 3 requires WordPress 6.9+. Use version 2 for 6.4-6.8.

faq.question: How does this skill integrate with other tools?
faq.answer: Works with wp-project-triage for project detection. WP-CLI may be needed for some workflows.

faq.question: Does this skill modify my files?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides guidance and references. It does not write to files or execute unsafe operations.

faq.question: Why are my theme.json styles not applying?
faq.answer: Check: correct theme edited, valid JSON syntax, user customizations not overriding, correct style hierarchy.

faq.question: How are style variations different from theme.json?
faq.answer: Style variations (styles/*.json) are user-selectable presets. Once selected, the choice is stored in the database.
